nodes.title: Inject clock for deterministic timing
nodes.why: A default clock keeps usage simple, and injection makes tests deterministic.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly wider API, but much better testability and flexibility.

nodes.title: Capture start time before running fn
nodes.why: Timing should include everything from invocation until the function settles.
nodes.tradeoff: Pre-capture gives accurate full duration for both sync and async paths.

nodes.title: Normalize sync and async functions in one path
nodes.why: This handles plain returns, thrown sync errors, and promises with consistent behavior.
nodes.alternative: Call `fn()` directly and branch if it returns a promise.
nodes.tradeoff: One compact path is easier to reason about, with tiny promise overhead.

nodes.title: Capture end after result settles
nodes.why: For async functions, this ensures duration includes awaited time, not just call setup.
nodes.tradeoff: Post-settle timing matches real user-perceived completion time.

nodes.title: Return both result and duration
nodes.why: Callers usually need both the function output and timing metadata together.
nodes.tradeoff: A richer return shape is more useful, but slightly more verbose for callers.
